content.title: Core Concepts
content.description:
Everything in Hobson is built from a small set of fundamental concepts. Understanding these is essential for working with the system effectively.

---

## Items

The **item** is Hobson's universal data structure. Notes, code, types, views, the viewport--all are items with identical structure:

```javascript
{
  id: String,              // UUID (memorable GUIDs for seeds)
  name?: String,           // Optional; required for code items
  type: String,            // UUID reference to type item
  created: Timestamp,      // Immutable creation time
  modified: Timestamp,     // Updated on each change
  children: ChildSpec[],   // Ordered array for composition
  content: Object          // Arbitrary key-value data
}
```

### Key Properties

**`id`** - Globally unique identifier. Seed items use memorable GUIDs (e.g., `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000` for atom). User items get random UUIDs.

**`type`** - Points to another item that defines what this item *is*. Every item must have a type. See [Types](#types) below.

**`children`** - Array of child references. Can be simple IDs or objects with view configuration:

```javascript
// Simple reference
children: ["child-uuid-1", "child-uuid-2"]

// With view configuration (spatial canvas)
children: [
  { id: "child-uuid", view: { x: 100, y: 50, width: 400, height: 300, z: 1 } }
]
```

**`content`** - Unconstrained key-value store. The kernel doesn't enforce schemas; interpretation is up to views and editors. Common fields include:
- `title` / `description` - For notes and documentation
- `code` - For executable items
- `for_type` - For views (which type this view renders)

### Everything Is an Item

This radical uniformity means:
- One storage system handles all data
- One validation system (type chain checking) applies everywhere
- One rendering system displays everything
- Any item can be linked, transcluded, or composed

---

## Types

Types are items that define what other items are. Every item's `type` field points to a type item, forming chains that terminate at [atom](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000).

### The Type Chain

```
my-note → note → type-definition → atom
my-view → view → code → type-definition → atom
```

Type chains enable:
- **View lookup**: Walk the chain to find a view for a type (see [`findView`](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=findView))
- **Capability detection**: Check if an item is code, a view, etc. (see [`typeChainIncludes`](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=typeChainIncludes))
- **Inheritance**: Parent type's views work for child types

### Seed Types

<!-- [BEGIN:seed-types] -->

| Type | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| [atom](item://00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Self-referential root; `atom.type === atom` |
| [type-definition](item://11111111-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Type for defining new types |
| [code](item://22222222-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Base type for executable items |
| [library](item://66666666-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Reusable code modules |
| [view](item://aaaaaaaa-0000-0000-0000-000000000000) | Items that render other items |

<!-- [END:seed-types] -->

### Creating a Type

```javascript
// In REPL
await api.set({
  id: crypto.randomUUID(),
  name: 'my-custom-type',
  type: api.IDS.TYPE_DEFINITION,
  children: [],
  content: {
    description: 'A custom type for...',
    required_fields: ['title']
  }
});
```

No restart required--items of the new type immediately work with existing views (falling back up the type chain).

---

## Code as Data

Code items are items whose type chain includes [code](item://22222222-0000-0000-0000-000000000000). Their `content.code` field contains JavaScript evaluated as an ES module.

### Code Item Types

| Type | Purpose | Key Export |
|------|---------|------------|
| **Library** | Reusable utilities | Any exports |
| **View** | Renders items | `render(item, api)` |
| **Kernel Module** | Core system code | Various |

### The Module System

Code items are loaded via [`api.require()`](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=require):

```javascript
// By name
const lib = await api.require('my-library');

// By ID
const lib = await api.require('uuid-of-code-item');
```

**Features:**
- **Caching**: Modules cached by ID with timestamp
- **Auto-invalidation**: Cache cleared when `modified` changes
- **Circular detection**: Throws descriptive error if cycle found
- **Error isolation**: Failed loads don't crash the system

See [kernel:module-system](item://33333333-4444-0000-0000-000000000000) for implementation details.

### Why Code as Items?

1. **Inspectable**: View and edit code through Hobson's UI
2. **Linkable**: Documentation can reference live code
3. **Versionable**: Modified timestamps enable cache invalidation
4. **Uniform**: Code follows the same patterns as all other data

---

## Views

Views are code items that render other items. They export a `render(item, api)` function that returns a DOM node.

### View Lookup

When rendering an item, the system finds a view via preference hierarchy (see [`resolveView`](item://33333333-5555-0000-0000-000000000000#code?region=resolveView)):

1. **Explicit**: View ID passed to `renderItem()`
2. **Item preference**: `item.preferredView`
3. **Type preference**: `typeItem.preferredView`
4. **Type chain**: Walk up `extends` chain, find view with matching `for_type`
5. **Fallback**: [system:default-view](item://aaaaaaaa-1111-0000-0000-000000000000)

### View Structure

```javascript
{
  id: "...",
  name: "my-custom-view",
  type: api.IDS.VIEW,
  content: {
    for_type: "type-uuid-this-view-renders",
    displayName: "My View",
    description: "Renders items as...",
    code: `
      export async function render(item, api) {
        return api.createElement('div', {}, [
          item.content.title || 'Untitled'
        ]);
      }
    `
  }
}
```

See [Views & Rendering](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000004) for the complete view system.

---

## Content Field Patterns

While `content` is unconstrained, common patterns have emerged:

### Notes

```javascript
content: {
  title: "Note Title",
  description: "Markdown content..."  // or 'body' in some items
}
```

### Code Items

```javascript
content: {
  description: "What this code does",
  code: "export function..."  
}
```

### Views

```javascript
content: {
  for_type: "type-uuid",
  displayName: "Human Name",
  description: "Documentation",
  code: "export async function render..."
}
```

### Types

```javascript
content: {
  description: "What this type represents",
  required_fields: ["title"]  // Optional schema hint
}
```

---

## The Description Field

Every item can have `content.description`--human-readable prose explaining what the item is or does. This enables:

- **Self-documenting code**: Views and libraries describe themselves
- **Transclusion**: `![](item://uuid#description)` embeds just the description
- **Search**: Descriptions are indexed for full-text search
- **Discovery**: Browse items by reading their descriptions

Write descriptions in Markdown with item:// links to create richly interconnected documentation.
